entries.title: git reset --hard discards uncommitted changes with no undo
symptoms: uncommitted edits gone after a reset
working tree reverted unexpectedly
entries.root_cause: `git reset --hard` overwrites the working tree and index to match the target commit, throwing away uncommitted changes. They are not in any commit, so reflog cannot recover them.
_aliases: lost my changes after git reset
git reset hard deleted my work

fix_code.code: git stash push -m wip   # safety net before a hard reset

entries.title: Commits made on a detached HEAD are orphaned after checkout
symptoms: my commits vanished after switching branches
You are in 'detached HEAD' state
entries.root_cause: Committing while HEAD is detached (e.g. after `git checkout <sha>`) creates commits no branch points to. Switching away leaves them unreferenced and eventually garbage-collected.

entries.title: Anthropic prompt caching silently does nothing below the minimum cacheable length
symptoms: cache_creation_input_tokens is 0
no cache hit despite cache_control set
prompt caching has no effect on cost
entries.root_cause: A `cache_control` breakpoint only creates a cache entry when the prefix up to that point meets the model's minimum: ~1024 tokens for Opus/Sonnet and ~2048 tokens for Haiku. Shorter prefixes are silently not cached.

entries.title: asyncio.run() raises when called from inside a running event loop
symptoms: RuntimeError: asyncio.run() cannot be called from a running event loop
This event loop is already running
entries.root_cause: `asyncio.run()` creates and manages a new loop; calling it while a loop is already running (Jupyter, an async framework, a nested call) errors.

entries.title: Bare 'except:' swallows KeyboardInterrupt and SystemExit
symptoms: Ctrl-C does not stop the program
process won't exit cleanly
entries.root_cause: A bare `except:` (or `except BaseException`) catches `KeyboardInterrupt` and `SystemExit`, which inherit from `BaseException`, not `Exception`. Control-C gets swallowed.

entries.title: Claude Code Edit errors because the file was not Read first in the session
symptoms: Edit failed: file has not been read
you must read the file before editing it
entries.root_cause: The Edit tool requires the file to have been Read earlier in the same session so the harness can verify the pre-edit content. Editing an unread file is refused.

entries.title: MCP client calls tools before initialize and gets no tool list
symptoms: tools/list returns empty before initialize
server rejects requests until initialized
entries.root_cause: The MCP lifecycle requires the `initialize` request/response handshake before any `tools/list` or `tools/call`. Skipping it yields empty or rejected responses.

entries.title: MCP resources are read-only data and are not the right primitive for actions
symptoms: client never 'runs' a resource
expected a side effect from a resource read
entries.root_cause: MCP `resources` expose readable content (files, records) addressed by URI; `tools` perform actions. Modeling an action as a resource means it never executes.

entries.title: An unquoted or empty variable in rm -rf can delete the filesystem root
symptoms: rm: it is dangerous to operate recursively on '/'
files outside the intended directory deleted
entries.root_cause: If a variable in `rm -rf "$DIR/"` is empty or unset, the command expands to `rm -rf /`. Word-splitting on unquoted variables compounds the danger.

entries.title: Assistant message prefill fails when the content ends with trailing whitespace
symptoms: final assistant content block must not end with trailing whitespace
messages: assistant message cannot have trailing whitespace
entries.root_cause: When you prefill the assistant turn (to constrain output), the Messages API rejects a final assistant text block that ends in whitespace or a newline.

entries.title: await inside Array.forEach does not wait; the loop finishes before the promises resolve
symptoms: code after a forEach runs before the async work finishes
unhandled promise rejections from a forEach callback
entries.root_cause: `Array.prototype.forEach` ignores the return value of its callback, so an `async` callback's promise is never awaited. The loop returns immediately.

entries.title: git push --force clobbers a teammate's commits; --force-with-lease would have refused
symptoms: a colleague's commits disappeared from the remote branch
remote history rewritten over someone else's work
entries.root_cause: `git push --force` overwrites the remote ref unconditionally, even if someone pushed after your last fetch. Their commits are lost from the branch tip.

entries.title: Using Bash grep/find instead of the Grep/Glob tools wastes context and misses .gitignore handling
symptoms: huge bash grep output floods context
slow ad-hoc find in a large repo
entries.root_cause: The dedicated Grep and Glob tools are optimized for code search (ripgrep-backed, respect ignore files, compact output). Shelling out to `grep -r`/`find` is slower and dumps noisy output into context.

entries.title: datetime.utcnow() returns a naive datetime and is deprecated
symptoms: DeprecationWarning: datetime.datetime.utcnow() is deprecated
naive datetime compared to aware datetime raises TypeError
entries.root_cause: `datetime.utcnow()` returns a timezone-naive object that nonetheless represents UTC, causing silent bugs and TypeErrors when mixed with aware datetimes. It is deprecated in modern Python.

entries.title: LangChain ChatPromptTemplate raises KeyError on literal curly braces in the template
symptoms: KeyError on a brace-wrapped token that was meant to be literal
ValueError: Single '{' encountered in format string
entries.root_cause: LangChain prompt templates use `{var}` for variables. Literal braces — JSON examples, code with `{}` — are parsed as variables and fail.

entries.title: Gemini system instruction must be a separate parameter, not a contents entry
symptoms: system instruction ignored when placed in contents
model does not follow the system prompt
entries.root_cause: Gemini takes the system prompt via `system_instruction` on the model/config, not as a turn in `contents`. A 'system' turn in `contents` is invalid or ignored.

entries.title: Claude Code Read/Edit require absolute paths, not relative ones
symptoms: file_path must be absolute
relative path rejected by Read/Edit
entries.root_cause: The Read, Edit, and Write tools require an absolute `file_path`. Relative paths are rejected.

entries.title: OpenAI streaming tool-call arguments arrive as fragments that must be concatenated
symptoms: json.decoder.JSONDecodeError when parsing tool call arguments
tool call arguments look truncated in streamed chunks
entries.root_cause: In a streamed completion, `tool_calls[].function.arguments` is delivered as a sequence of string deltas across many chunks. Parsing any single chunk's fragment as JSON fails.

entries.title: Filenames with spaces break a for loop over unquoted command substitution
symptoms: loop iterates over fragments of a filename
No such file or directory for a name that exists
entries.root_cause: `for f in $(ls)` and unquoted `$(...)` split on whitespace, so a file named `my file.txt` becomes two iterations.

entries.title: Piping a remote script straight into a shell runs unverified code as you
symptoms: arbitrary code execution from a compromised or MITM'd installer URL
entries.root_cause: `curl … | sh` executes whatever the server returns, with no chance to inspect it, and a partial download can execute half a script. A compromised host or hijacked TLS becomes code execution on your machine.

entries.title: Dockerfile busts the layer cache by copying source before installing dependencies
symptoms: every build reinstalls all dependencies
slow Docker builds on small code changes
entries.root_cause: `COPY . .` before the dependency install means any source change invalidates the install layer, so dependencies reinstall on every build.

entries.title: OpenAI JSON mode errors unless the word 'json' appears in the messages
symptoms: 'messages' must contain the word 'json' in some form to use 'response_format' of type 'json_object'
entries.root_cause: `response_format={'type':'json_object'}` requires the string `json` to appear somewhere in the prompt, as a guard against silent infinite-whitespace generation.

entries.title: LangChain output truncated because max_tokens defaulted too low on the chat model
symptoms: completions are cut short through LangChain but fine via the raw SDK
long generations stop early
entries.root_cause: Some LangChain chat-model wrappers set a conservative default `max_tokens`. The cap silently truncates long outputs even though the underlying API would allow more.

entries.title: OpenAI embeddings call fails when a single input exceeds the model token limit
symptoms: maximum context length is 8192 tokens, however you requested ...
400 on embeddings input
entries.root_cause: Embedding models cap input at ~8192 tokens per item. Long documents must be chunked before embedding.
_aliases: text too big to embed in one request
context length exceeded on embeddings endpoint

entries.title: MCP tool handler returns a bare string instead of a content list
symptoms: validation error: content is not a list
client shows nothing for a tool that 'worked'
entries.root_cause: An MCP `call_tool` handler must return a list of content blocks (e.g. `TextContent`), not a raw string or dict. A bare return value fails validation or renders as empty.

entries.title: Claude Code Bash: exported env vars and cd do not persist across separate Bash calls for shell state
symptoms: environment variable set in one command is empty in the next
a function defined earlier is not found later
entries.root_cause: Each Bash tool call starts a fresh shell initialized from the profile. The working directory persists between calls, but shell state — exported env vars, shell functions, `set` options — does not.

entries.title: Output truncated silently because stop_reason was max_tokens, not end_turn
symptoms: response cut off mid-sentence
JSON output from the model fails to parse
stop_reason: max_tokens
entries.root_cause: When generation hits the `max_tokens` ceiling, the API returns a complete, successful response whose `stop_reason` is `max_tokens`. The text is truncated but no error is raised.

entries.title: OpenAI reasoning models reject temperature and top_p
symptoms: Unsupported value: 'temperature' does not support 0.7 with this model. Only the default (1) value is supported.
400 on o1/o3 when sampling params are set
entries.root_cause: Reasoning models do not accept custom `temperature`, `top_p`, `presence_penalty`, or `frequency_penalty`; only default values are allowed.

entries.title: Anthropic tool_result must be in a user message and reference the matching tool_use_id
symptoms: tool_result block(s) provided when previous message does not contain tool_use
Each tool_result must have a tool_use_id matching a prior tool_use
entries.root_cause: Tool results are delivered as a `tool_result` content block inside a `user` message, and each must carry the `tool_use_id` of the corresponding `tool_use` block from the previous assistant turn.

entries.title: Anthropic vision request fails without media_type on a base64 image source
symptoms: messages.X.content.Y.image.source.media_type: Field required
image not accepted
entries.root_cause: An image content block with `source.type=base64` requires both `media_type` (e.g. image/png) and base64 `data`. Omitting `media_type` is rejected.

entries.title: Anthropic Messages API request fails because max_tokens was omitted
symptoms: max_tokens: Field required
TypeError: create() missing 1 required keyword-only argument: 'max_tokens'
entries.root_cause: Unlike some APIs where the token cap is optional, the Anthropic Messages API requires `max_tokens` on every request.

entries.title: OpenAI reasoning models reject max_tokens; they require max_completion_tokens
symptoms: Unsupported parameter: 'max_tokens' is not supported with this model. Use 'max_completion_tokens' instead.
400 invalid_request_error on o1/o3/o4 calls
entries.root_cause: Reasoning models (o1, o3, o4 family) count hidden reasoning tokens toward output and replaced `max_tokens` with `max_completion_tokens`, which bounds reasoning + visible output together.

entries.title: OpenAI 'system' role is rejected by reasoning models, which expect 'developer'
symptoms: Unsupported value: 'messages[0].role' does not support 'system' with this model
developer role expected
entries.root_cause: Reasoning models renamed the steering role from `system` to `developer`. Sending `role: system` to them is rejected.

entries.title: Anthropic Messages API rejects a system prompt sent as a role:system message
symptoms: messages: Input should be 'user' or 'assistant'
system role is not supported in the messages array
entries.root_cause: The Messages API takes the system prompt as a top-level `system` parameter, not as an entry in the `messages` array. Only `user` and `assistant` roles are valid inside `messages`.

entries.title: Claude Code Edit tool fails when old_string includes the line-number prefix from Read output
symptoms: Edit failed: string not found in file
old_string does not match
entries.root_cause: Read tool output uses `cat -n` style line-number prefixes (line number + tab). Agents naively copy that into old_string, but the actual file content does not contain the prefix, so the match fails.

entries.title: Retrying an Anthropic 529 overloaded_error without backoff makes the overload worse
symptoms: 529 overloaded_error
Error: Overloaded
entries.root_cause: A 529 means the service is temporarily overloaded. Tight retry loops add load and prolong the failure.

entries.title: Missing .dockerignore sends the whole working tree as build context
symptoms: docker build is slow and uploads gigabytes of context
node_modules or .git copied into the image
entries.root_cause: Without a `.dockerignore`, `docker build` sends everything in the context dir to the daemon, and `COPY . .` can pull in `.git`, `node_modules`, caches, and secrets.

entries.title: Ignoring Retry-After on a 429 makes rate limiting worse
symptoms: 429 Too Many Requests in a tight loop
ban or longer backoff from the server
entries.root_cause: A `429` (and some `503`) responses include a `Retry-After` header telling you when to retry. Ignoring it and hammering extends the limit window.

entries.title: set -e does not abort on a failing command in the middle of a pipeline
symptoms: script continues after a piped command failed
exit code of the pipeline is 0 despite an upstream failure
entries.root_cause: Under `set -e`, only the pipeline's last command's exit status matters by default. A failure in `cmd1 | cmd2` is hidden if `cmd2` succeeds.

entries.title: Extended thinking breaks on the next turn when prior thinking blocks are dropped
symptoms: messages.X.content: thinking block is required
Expected `thinking` block before tool_use when extended thinking is enabled
entries.root_cause: When extended thinking is enabled together with tool use, the assistant's `thinking` (and `redacted_thinking`) blocks from a turn must be passed back verbatim in the conversation history for subsequent turns. Stripping them to 'save tokens' invalidates the turn.

entries.title: Blindly retrying non-idempotent HTTP requests duplicates side effects
symptoms: duplicate charges or records after a retry
double POST on timeout
entries.root_cause: Retrying a POST/PATCH that timed out can execute the action twice if the first attempt actually succeeded server-side. Only idempotent methods (GET, PUT, DELETE) are safe to retry blindly.

entries.title: JSON.parse returns any, erasing type safety on external data
symptoms: runtime shape errors on parsed JSON that 'typechecked'
undefined property access on API responses
entries.root_cause: `JSON.parse` is typed as returning `any`, so the compiler trusts whatever type you assert. Malformed or unexpected payloads pass typechecking and explode at runtime.

entries.title: Claude Code Edit fails when old_string is not unique in the file
symptoms: Edit failed: old_string appears multiple times
found N matches for old_string; expected 1
entries.root_cause: The Edit tool replaces a single unique occurrence by default. If `old_string` matches more than once, it refuses rather than guess.

entries.title: Setting seed does not guarantee identical OpenAI completions
symptoms: outputs differ run-to-run despite a fixed seed
entries.root_cause: `seed` makes sampling best-effort reproducible but is not a hard guarantee; backend changes shift `system_fingerprint` and outputs can still vary.

entries.title: process.env values are always strings, so numeric and boolean comparisons mislead
symptoms: PORT comparison or arithmetic behaves oddly
process.env.DEBUG === true is always false
entries.root_cause: Every `process.env.X` is a string or undefined. `process.env.FLAG === true` is always false; `process.env.PORT + 1` concatenates.

entries.title: git clean -fdx deletes untracked files including ignored ones like .env and build caches
symptoms: .env or local config deleted by git clean
untracked but important files removed
entries.root_cause: `git clean -fdx` removes all untracked files and directories, and `-x` additionally removes git-ignored files — which often includes local `.env`, credentials, and caches you wanted to keep.

entries.title: Python mutable default argument is shared across all calls
symptoms: a list/dict default 'remembers' values between calls
unexpected accumulation in a default argument
entries.root_cause: Default argument values are evaluated once at function definition, so a mutable default (`[]`, `{}`) is shared by every call that uses the default.

entries.title: OpenAI tool-call arguments are a JSON string, not a dict
symptoms: TypeError: string indices must be integers
treating arguments as a dict raises
entries.root_cause: `tool_calls[].function.arguments` is always a JSON-encoded string, even in non-streaming responses. It is never pre-parsed into an object.

entries.title: MCP stdio server corrupts the protocol by writing logs to stdout
symptoms: client fails to parse server message
Unexpected token in JSON
MCP handshake hangs or errors after a print() call
entries.root_cause: An MCP stdio server speaks JSON-RPC over stdout. Any stray `print()` or library log that writes to stdout interleaves with protocol frames and corrupts them.
_aliases: garbage before JSON in MCP stdio transport

entries.title: Gemini returns empty text because the response was blocked by default safety settings
symptoms: response.text raises because no Part was returned
finish_reason: SAFETY
candidate has no content
entries.root_cause: Gemini applies safety filters by default. When a category trips, the candidate is returned with `finish_reason=SAFETY` and no text part, so accessing `.text` raises.

entries.title: MCP tool inputSchema must be a JSON Schema object with type 'object' at the root
symptoms: tool arguments not validated or passed
client cannot construct a form for the tool
entries.root_cause: A tool's `inputSchema` must be a JSON Schema whose root `type` is `object` with a `properties` map. A non-object root (array, string) or a missing `type` prevents clients from building/validating arguments.

entries.title: Gemini API rejects role 'assistant'; the model role is 'model'
symptoms: Please use a valid role: user, model.
400 INVALID_ARGUMENT on contents role
entries.root_cause: The Gemini API uses `user` and `model` as the two conversation roles. `assistant` (the OpenAI/Anthropic name) is invalid.

entries.title: CMD in shell form does not forward SIGTERM, so the container ignores graceful shutdown
symptoms: container takes 10s to stop then is SIGKILLed
app never receives SIGTERM
entries.root_cause: Shell-form `CMD app` runs the process as a child of `/bin/sh`, which is PID 1 and does not forward signals. `docker stop` sends SIGTERM to PID 1 (the shell), not your app.

entries.title: Gemini multi-turn fails when user and model turns do not strictly alternate
symptoms: Please ensure that multiturn requests alternate between user and model.
400 on contents with consecutive same-role turns
entries.root_cause: Gemini requires `contents` to alternate user/model. Two consecutive user turns (e.g. a system-ish preamble plus the real question both as `user`) are rejected.
